agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive delivery with a 2-hour window, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-bound delivery commitments, driver eligibility constraints, real-time tracking, and a distinct refund/compensation policy.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available only to eligible customers within a defined service radius." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A surcharge of $12.99 applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery tier." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour time span beginning at order placement within which the express delivery must be completed." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "The condition where a delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering the refund policy." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The requirement that an express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement." },
      { "term": "Service Radius", "definition": "The maximum distance (10km) between the customer's delivery address and the nearest warehouse, used to determine express eligibility." },
      { "term": "Driver Eligibility (Express)", "definition": "A driver qualifies for express assignments only if they hold a rating of 4.0 stars or above." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A discretionary (non-contractual) refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed, subject to exclusion conditions." },
      { "term": "Driver Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute window within which a replacement driver must be assigned after a mid-delivery driver cancellation, failing which an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "A refund exclusion condition where no goodwill refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical/logistical constraint — offering express outside this radius makes the 2-hour window physically unachievable and corrupts the service promise.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system must reject express delivery selection when the calculated distance between the delivery address and the nearest warehouse exceeds 10km."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that Finance can adjust. The amount is not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects Express Delivery, the order total includes a line item of $12.99 labeled as the express fee, added on top of the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Exceeding 15 minutes for pickup makes the 2-hour delivery window operationally unachievable. This is a hard operational constraint tied to the physics of the delivery chain.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes of placement, the system must escalate (e.g., alert operations, attempt reassignment). The order must not silently remain unassigned."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above are eligible for express delivery assignments.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Rating threshold is an operational policy that Ops leadership could adjust based on driver supply and demand.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system must not assign an express delivery to a driver whose current rating is below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When a delivery window is missed, only the express fee is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund scope is a business policy decision that could be revised by Finance or PM.",
      "testable_assertion": "A missed-window refund must equal exactly the express fee amount ($12.99) and must not include the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong delivery address, even if the delivery window is missed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a policy exclusion that could be changed by Customer Success or Legal.",
      "testable_assertion": "When the delivery window is missed and the order is flagged as 'wrong address provided by customer', the system must not issue a goodwill refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Goodwill refunds for missed windows are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is an operational/finance policy, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A goodwill refund record must have a target processing date no later than 3 business days from the missed-window event timestamp."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute replacement window is an operational target that could be adjusted.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a driver cancels an in-progress express delivery, the system must attempt reassignment immediately and track elapsed time. If 10 minutes elapse without reassignment, an automatic refund must be triggered."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "If no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a mid-delivery cancellation, the express fee is refunded automatically.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Automatic refund trigger is a customer success policy, not a system invariant.",
      "testable_assertion": "When the 10-minute replacement window expires without a new driver assigned, the system must create an automatic goodwill refund for the express fee without requiring manual approval."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "Express deliveries must provide real-time driver location tracking to the customer.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Real-time tracking is a core capability of the Express product — without it, the product is fundamentally incomplete. You cannot sell a time-sensitive delivery without visibility.",
      "testable_assertion": "Once a driver is assigned to an express order, the customer-facing interface must display the driver's current location, updated at intervals no greater than 30 seconds."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10.0km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — the boundary condition needs explicit definition: is 10km inclusive or exclusive? Recommend inclusive (<=10km)." },
    { "scenario": "Driver's rating drops below 4.0 stars while en route on an express delivery", "expected_behavior": "The delivery should be completed by the current driver. Rating eligibility is checked at assignment time, not continuously during transit." },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 11:50 PM — 2-hour window spans midnight", "expected_behavior": "The delivery window is clock-agnostic — 2 hours from placement regardless of midnight crossing. Business-day logic for refunds starts the next business day if window expires on a weekend/holiday." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after placing an express order", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — no stakeholder addressed this. If the new address is outside 10km, should the express option be revoked? Does address change reset the 2-hour window?" },
    { "scenario": "No eligible drivers available within 15 minutes (driver supply shortage)", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the order be downgraded to standard delivery with a proactive refund? Or held and attempted? Operations did not define the fallback path." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 110 of a 120-minute window — replacement window extends past delivery window", "expected_behavior": "The 10-minute replacement window still applies but the delivery window will almost certainly be missed. Both the replacement-failure refund (BR-9) and the missed-window refund (BR-5) should not stack — only one express fee refund should be issued." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a correct address but is unreachable (no answer, gated community)", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does this count as a customer-caused failure like 'wrong address'? Refund exclusion scope is unclear." },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse is temporarily closed or out of stock for express items", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should express option be suppressed dynamically based on warehouse availability?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one is used for distance calculation?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should it be the nearest warehouse, or the warehouse that has the items in stock? This affects eligibility." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream. Goodwill refunds are a direct cost center — without the 'wrong address' exclusion and clear refund triggers, refund abuse is possible. Double-refund risk (missed window + failed replacement) must be prevented. 3-business-day refund processing has cash flow implications.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA requires dedicated express driver pools or priority dispatch logic. 4-star driver requirement constrains the eligible driver pool — if supply is thin, pickup SLAs will be missed, triggering refunds. Real-time tracking requires GPS infrastructure investment. The 10-minute replacement window demands always-on dispatch capacity.",
    "user": "Customers get a premium, time-guaranteed experience with full visibility. However, the legal 'best effort' framing directly conflicts with the prominent '2-hour guarantee' messaging CS wants — this creates trust risk if customers feel misled. Refund exclusions (wrong address) must be communicated transparently to avoid support escalations.",
    "risk": "The guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction is the highest risk item. Displaying a 'guarantee' that Legal says cannot be a guarantee exposes the company to false advertising claims. Additionally, the lack of a fallback path when no express drivers are available creates a scenario where orders are accepted but cannot be fulfilled.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with time commitment and real-time tracking. Risk of confusion if 'guarantee' language is used but refunds are discretionary." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "New express tier with stricter eligibility (4-star+), tighter SLAs (15-min pickup), and higher operational pressure. May need incentive structure." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "Must build priority dispatch, 15-minute escalation flows, 10-minute replacement flows, and real-time monitoring dashboards." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue line (express fees), new cost line (goodwill refunds), need refund tracking and abuse detection." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle escalations for missed windows, refund disputes (especially wrong-address exclusions), and 'guarantee' complaints." },
      { "group": "Legal / Compliance", "impact": "Must approve all customer-facing language. 'Guarantee' wording must be replaced or qualified to avoid liability." },
      { "group": "Engineering", "impact": "Must implement distance calculation, real-time tracking, time-based SLA monitoring, automatic refund triggers, and driver eligibility filtering." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Critical contradiction between PM/CS ('guaranteed 2-hour delivery shown prominently') and Legal ('best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee, cannot guarantee in writing'). This is not a minor wording issue — it fundamentally changes the product's value proposition, customer-facing messaging, refund policy framing, and compliance posture. Additionally, multiple operational gaps remain unresolved (no-driver fallback, address changes, warehouse availability). Until the guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction is resolved at the executive level, acceptance criteria cannot be finalized.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) — all parties agree",
      "10km service radius — stated clearly by PM, unchallenged",
      "15-minute pickup SLA — clear operational constraint",
      "4-star driver eligibility — clear operational policy",
      "Wrong address exclusion from refund — clear Finance policy",
      "Real-time tracking requirement — clear CS requirement"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a 'guarantee' or 'best effort' — fundamental product identity unresolved",
      "Customer-facing language for the delivery promise",
      "Whether refund is automatic or discretionary (PM says refund on miss; Legal says goodwill gesture)",
      "Fallback path when no express-eligible drivers are available",
      "Address change handling after express order placement",
      "Whether the 10-minute replacement auto-refund interacts with the missed-window refund (double refund risk)",
      "Warehouse selection logic for multi-warehouse scenarios"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance — this was not specified",
      "Driver rating is the lifetime/rolling average, not a per-day or per-week rating — rating calculation method was not specified",
      "The 2-hour delivery window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time — PM said 'within 2 hours' without specifying the start event",
      "A single express fee refund is the maximum compensation per order — no stakeholder mentioned double refunds",
      "'3 business days' for refund processing means initiation, not settlement to customer's payment method"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' that should be shown prominently to customers. Legal explicitly states this cannot be a guarantee in writing — it must be framed as 'best effort' with the refund being a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These positions are mutually exclusive and affect every customer-facing touchpoint, the refund policy's legal standing, and the core product identity.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal / Compliance", "Customer Support", "Product Management"],
      "options": [
        "A: Use 'guarantee' language — accept legal risk, make refund contractually enforceable",
        "B: Use 'best effort' language — reduce legal risk, but weakens the product's market appeal and customer trust",
        "C: Hybrid — use aspirational language ('our express promise') without the word 'guarantee', offer refunds as a standing policy but not a contractual right"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Use 'Express Promise' or 'Express Commitment' language that conveys reliability without creating a contractual guarantee. Define the refund as a standing policy in Terms of Service with explicit exclusions, reviewable by the company. This satisfies Legal's concerns while preserving the customer value proposition. Requires executive sign-off."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "No stakeholder defined what happens when an express order is accepted but no eligible driver (4-star+) is available within the 15-minute pickup window. This is a critical operational gap — the system needs a defined fallback: auto-downgrade to standard? Cancel and refund? Hold and keep trying?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations / Dispatch", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-downgrade to standard delivery with proactive express fee refund and customer notification",
        "B: Continue attempting express dispatch beyond 15 minutes, accepting likely window miss",
        "C: Prevent express order acceptance when driver supply is predicted to be insufficient"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A for immediate launch (auto-downgrade with proactive refund and transparent notification). Option C as a future enhancement (predictive availability gating). Needs PM and Ops alignment."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The start time of the 2-hour delivery window is undefined. PM says 'within 2 hours' — but from when? Order placement? Payment confirmation? Driver assignment? This has major implications for whether the window is achievable given the 15-minute pickup SLA.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations / Dispatch", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Window starts at order placement (strictest — gives operations only 105 minutes after the 15-min pickup SLA)",
        "B: Window starts at payment confirmation (slightly more lenient if payment processing has latency)",
        "C: Window starts at driver pickup (most lenient for operations but least valuable to the customer)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — the customer's perception of time starts when they place the order. Any other definition creates a gap between customer expectation and operational measurement, leading to disputes."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Distance calculation method (straight-line vs. driving distance) and warehouse selection logic (nearest vs. stocked) are undefined. In dense urban areas, 10km straight-line could mean 20km+ driving distance, making the 2-hour window unachievable.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations / Dispatch", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Use straight-line distance (simpler, more permissive, higher miss rate)",
        "B: Use driving distance (more accurate, requires routing API, reduces eligible area)",
        "C: Use straight-line with a conservative buffer (e.g., 8km straight-line ≈ 10km driving)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — driving distance. The 10km constraint exists to ensure deliverability. Straight-line distance undermines that purpose. Use the nearest warehouse that can fulfill the order."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed express delivery availability hours (24/7? business hours only?), item eligibility (are all items express-eligible or only certain categories?), or order value limits (minimum/maximum for express). These are fundamental product scoping questions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Product Management", "Operations / Dispatch"],
      "options": [
        "A: Launch with 24/7 availability, all items eligible, no order limits",
        "B: Launch with restricted hours and eligible item categories, expand later",
        "C: Gather additional requirements from PM and Ops before defining"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — these are product-defining decisions that cannot be assumed. Escalate to PM for explicit scoping."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
